01_initial_request.input_data: protein sequences from the MGnify90 database
01_initial_request.user_intent: To perform evolutionary-scale structural characterization of metagenomic proteins to explore vast biological diversity and enable discoveries in medicine and biotechnology
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Fold >617 million protein sequences resulting in >225 million high-confidence structure predictions

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein structure prediction traditionally requires multiple sequence alignments (MSAs) which are computationally expensive. [Gap] Need to determine if language models can learn structural information directly from sequences without MSAs. [Decision] To achieve evolutionary-scale structure prediction, chose to use ESM-2 (transformer protein language model) to train models at scales from 8 million to 15 billion parameters on UniRef database, expecting to observe emergence of structural information in representations.

parameters.training_database: UniRef (~65 million unique sequences from ~138 million UniRef90 sequences)
parameters.training_objective: masked language modeling (15% of positions masked)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Atomic-resolution structure emerges in language model representations. 15 billion parameter model reaches perplexity of 6.37 (vs 10.45 for 8M model). Long-range contact precision improves with scale. TM-score of 0.72 on CAMEO and 0.55 on CASP14 for 15B model.

references.relevance: Primary source describing ESM-2 training and emergence of structure

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ESM-2 language models show structural information in representations but need a folding head to output 3D coordinates. [Gap] Need to convert language model representations to atomic-level structure predictions. [Decision] To achieve end-to-end structure prediction, chose to use ESMFold (folding head trained on ESM-2) to train on experimental and predicted structures, expecting to obtain accurate single-sequence structure predictor.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ESMFold achieves average TM-score of 0.83 on CAMEO and 0.68 on CASP14. Speed improvement of up to 60x over AlphaFold2 on short sequences. Confidence (pLDDT) well-calibrated with accuracy.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ESMFold trained but needs validation against held-out test sets to assess accuracy. [Gap] Need to evaluate prediction quality before large-scale application. [Decision] To achieve quality assessment, chose to use ESMFold to evaluate on CAMEO and CASP14 test sets, expecting to obtain TM-scores and LDDT metrics for comparison with AlphaFold2.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ESMFold achieves TM-score 0.83 on CAMEO (vs AlphaFold2 0.88) and 0.68 on CASP14 (vs AlphaFold2 0.85). On subset with perplexity <7, ESMFold matches AlphaFold performance. pLDDT correlates with accuracy (Pearson -0.52 on CAMEO, -0.71 on CASP14).

references.relevance: Validation results on benchmark test sets

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ESMFold validated and shows speed advantage over MSA-based methods. [Gap] Need to apply to metagenomic scale to characterize vast protein diversity. [Decision] To achieve evolutionary-scale structural characterization, chose to use ESMFold to predict structures for all sequences in MGnify90 database, expecting to fold >617 million proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Completed structural characterization of >617 million metagenomic protein sequences. Produced ~365 million predictions with good confidence (mean pLDDT >0.5 and pTM >0.5) and ~225 million with high confidence (mean pLDDT >0.7 and pTM >0.7).

references.citation: Mitchell et al., Nucleic Acids Res. 2020 (ref 32)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High-confidence predictions generated but need to assess novelty compared to known structures. [Gap] Need to determine how many predicted structures are distinct from existing PDB structures. [Decision] To achieve structural novelty assessment, chose to use Foldseek to compare predicted structures to PDB, expecting to identify structures with low TM-score to known proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: At TM-score 0.7 threshold, 25.4% (253,905 proteins) without match. At TM-score 0.5 threshold, 12.6% (125,765 proteins) without match. ~28 million proteins (12.6% of 225 million) have high-confidence predictions and TM-score <0.5 to known structures.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Structural novelty assessed but need to evaluate sequence-level novelty as well. [Gap] Need to determine how many predicted proteins are distinct from existing UniRef90 sequences. [Decision] To achieve sequence novelty assessment, chose to use blastp to compare predicted sequences to UniRef90, expecting to identify proteins with low sequence identity to known sequences.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 76.8% (767,580) of proteins have sequence identity below 90% to any sequence in UniRef90. For 3.4% (33,521 proteins), no match found in UniRef90 at all.

references.citation: Altschul et al., J. Mol. Biol. 1990 (ref 60)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Calibration of confidence scores critical for large-scale predictions. [Gap] Need to verify ESMFold confidence correlates with actual accuracy on metagenomic sequences. [Decision] To achieve confidence calibration, chose to use AlphaFold2 as reference to compare ESMFold pLDDT against LDDT on metagenomic subset, expecting to validate confidence scores.

parameters.comparison_metric: pLDDT vs LDDT to AlphaFold2 predictions

02_agent_trajectory.observation: High correlation (Pearson r=0.79) between ESMFold pLDDT and LDDT to AlphaFold2 predictions. When pLDDT >0.9, ESMFold predictions approach experimental accuracy (median all-atom RMSD95 of 1.42 Å).

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Computational validation using benchmark test sets (CAMEO, CASP14) and comparison to AlphaFold2 predictions on metagenomic sequences

total_sequences_folded.interpretation: Exceeds target of folding >617 million protein sequences from MGnify90

high_confidence_predictions.interpretation: Meets target of >225 million high-confidence structure predictions (mean pLDDT >0.7 and pTM >0.7)

cameo_tm_score.interpretation: Competitive accuracy compared to AlphaFold2 (0.88) on CAMEO test set

casp14_tm_score.interpretation: Reasonable accuracy on difficult CASP14 targets compared to AlphaFold2 (0.85)

structural_novelty.interpretation: 12.6% of high-confidence predictions ( ~28 million) have TM-score <0.5 to known PDB structures, indicating novel structural space

sequence_novelty.interpretation: 76.8% of high-confidence predictions are separate from UniRef90 by at least 90% sequence identity

speed_improvement.interpretation: ESMFold achieves up to 60x speedup over AlphaFold2 on short sequences, enabling large-scale prediction

computation_time.interpretation: Completed on cluster of ~2000 GPUs, demonstrating scalability

03_success_verification.final_verdict: SUCCESS - The research objective was fully achieved. The study successfully folded >617 million protein sequences from MGnify90 database, resulting in >225 million high-confidence structure predictions. The ESMFold method demonstrated competitive accuracy with AlphaFold2 while achieving order-of-magnitude speedup, enabling evolutionary-scale structural characterization of metagenomic proteins. The ESM Metagenomic Atlas was created as an open science resource.
